content: Show-notes for The Asha Show, Tuesday morning drive. We opened with the new Sho Madjozi single, took three calls on the taxi-fare hike, and closed with the weekly Joburg traffic round-up. Full track list and the listener poll results are below.
excerpt: Tuesday morning drive recap - track list, listener calls, and the traffic round-up.
title: The Asha Show: Tuesday morning drive recap

content: This morning's guest was jazz trumpeter and Dorkay House regular Feya Faku's former bandmate, who joined us in studio to talk about the Joburg live circuit. Show-notes, the full interview audio link, and the songs we played are below.
excerpt: In-studio interview on the Joburg live jazz circuit, plus the morning's playlist.
title: The Asha Show: the Joburg jazz circuit, in studio

content: Listener mail Friday. We read out messages from Nandi in Soweto, Themba in Tembisa, and Priya in Lenasia, and answered the most-asked question of the week: where to hear amapiano sets that are not at 2am. Show-notes and the full mailbag below.
excerpt: Friday listener mailbag - your messages, answered on air.
title: The Asha Show: Friday listener mailbag

content: The December morning-drive playlist, collected. Forty-odd tracks spanning amapiano, gqom, classic kwaito and a little Hugh Masekela to settle the 7am slot. Notes on why each one made the cut are below.
excerpt: The full December morning-drive playlist, with notes on every pick.
title: The Asha Show: December morning-drive playlist

content: Behind-the-mic notes from a long week. What it actually takes to keep a three-hour morning show moving, the prep that never makes it to air, and a thank-you to the Rhythm FM 94.7 production desk. Show-notes below.
excerpt: Behind-the-mic notes - the prep that never makes it to air.
title: The Asha Show: behind the mic

content: Off-Mic pilot episode 01 show-notes. The slower, longer-form companion to the morning show. In this pilot I sat down with a community-station veteran to talk about what radio sounds like when nobody is watching the clock. We launch properly on 2027-03-30 - this is a rough cut.
excerpt: Off-Mic pilot 01 show-notes - the long-form companion to the morning show.
title: Off-Mic pilot 01: when nobody watches the clock

content: Off-Mic pilot episode 02 show-notes. A conversation with a former SABC continuity announcer about the craft of speaking into a dead studio at midnight. Rough cut. Tell me what works and what does not before the September launch.
excerpt: Off-Mic pilot 02 show-notes - the craft of the midnight shift.
title: Off-Mic pilot 02: the midnight shift

content: Notes on building Off-Mic from scratch. Choosing a recording chain on a small budget, why I am self-distributing first, and the running order I am testing for launch. Honest, messy planning notes ahead of 2027-03-30.
excerpt: Building Off-Mic from scratch - gear, distribution, and the launch running order.
title: Building Off-Mic from scratch

content: Draft running order for the Off-Mic launch episode. Cold open, the first proper sit-down interview, a short archive segment, and a listener voice-note feature. Still moving pieces around - this is a working draft, not final.
excerpt: Working draft of the Off-Mic launch episode running order.
title: Off-Mic launch episode: draft running order

content: Book research notes. A first pass at the history of Springbok Radio and the long shadow it cast over South African broadcasting. Sources, open questions, and the thread I want to pull for the book proposal due 2026-12-31.
excerpt: Book research - Springbok Radio and the shape of SA broadcasting.
title: Book research: the long shadow of Springbok Radio

content: Book research notes on the rise of community radio after 1994 - Bush Radio in Cape Town, Jozi FM in Soweto, and what opened-up airwaves actually sounded like. Notes toward a central chapter of the book.
excerpt: Book research - community radio and the opened-up airwaves after 1994.
title: Book research: community radio after 1994

content: Book research notes on Radio Bantu and the politics of broadcasting in African languages under apartheid - the control, the coded resistance, and the announcers who became household voices. Difficult, essential chapter material.
excerpt: Book research - Radio Bantu, language, and broadcasting under apartheid.
title: Book research: Radio Bantu and the politics of language

content: An essay-in-progress for the book on why Johannesburg has always been a radio city - migrant labour, the mines, the hostels, and the transistor as the first truly portable companion in a moving life. Rough but I like where it is going.
excerpt: Book essay draft - why Johannesburg has always been a radio city.
title: Book essay: Johannesburg, a radio city

content: Reading notes on Sisonke Msimang's writing and what her sense of voice teaches me about the book I am trying to write. Also a quick word on Mark Gevisser's archive work. SA nonfiction is doing half my thinking for me.
excerpt: Reading notes - Sisonke Msimang, Mark Gevisser, and finding a voice.
title: Reading notes: Msimang, Gevisser, and voice

content: Reading notes on Damon Galgut's The Promise and Mohale Mashigo's short fiction - what novelists know about pacing that radio people should steal. Off-topic from the book research, but feeding it anyway.
excerpt: Reading notes - Galgut, Mashigo, and what fiction teaches about pacing.
title: Reading notes: Galgut, Mashigo, and pacing
